bids.reasoning: As this is the opening offer at the very beginning of the negotiation, I will start by proposing my most preferred values for all issues. This serves to maximize my potential utility and sets a strong initial anchor from which I can steadily make considered concessions in subsequent rounds.

The negotiation has just started (time fraction 0.02) and the opponent has countered with a bid that maximizes their utility at the cost of mine. To show a willingness to negotiate while prioritizing my highest-weighted issues, I will maintain my positions on issueD (weight 0.4), issueC (weight 0.3), and issueB (weight 0.2) and make a minor concession on issueA (weight 0.1). This step starts the concession process cautiously.

We are very early in the negotiation (t = 0.04) and the opponent has simply repeated their opening offer of all least-preferred values for me. To keep the negotiation moving, I will incrementally concede on my lowest-priority issue (issueA) while holding firm on my most important issues (issueC and issueD) to protect my overall utility and signal my core priorities.

The negotiation is still very early (t=0.06), but the opponent has repeatedly offered their most extreme stance without conceding at all. To signal willingness to negotiate while protecting the high-value issues (issueC and issueD), I will concede further on the lowest-weight issue (issueA) down to valueC, hoping to prompt a reciprocal concession from them.

The negotiation is still in its very early stages (t=0.08). The opponent has repeatedly proposed their presumed optimal outcome without making any concessions. I have gradually conceded on my least important issue (issueA) to demonstrate flexibility and encourage reciprocity. I will make one more concession on issueA, reducing it to valueB, while holding completely firm on my highest-priority issues (issueD, issueC, and issueB) to invite a counter-offer that bridges the gap.

The time fraction is still very early at 0.1, but the opponent is repeatedly insisting on their opening offer. I have steadily conceded on issueA, my least important issue. I will now concede completely on issueA to see if the opponent is willing to move on the other issues, while holding firm on my highest-priority issues (D, C, and B).

The negotiation is still in its early stages (t=0.12), but the opponent has stubbornly maintained their initial offer without making any concessions. I have already conceded my least important issue (issueA) down to their preferred value. I will now start conceding incrementally on my next lowest-weight issue, issueB, dropping from valueF to valueE, while holding firm on my most important issues (issueC and issueD) to see if they start responding.

I have been gradually yielding on my lower-weighted issues (A and B) while the opponent remains completely rigid, repeatedly demanding valueA for all issues without any concessions or communication. Because it is still early in the negotiation (t=0.14), I will concede slightly further down on my second-lowest priority issue (issueB) to valueD, while maintaining my ideal positions on my highest-priority issues (issueC and issueD) to protect my overall utility.

The opponent continues to repeat their opening bid without making any concessions, remaining at the absolute minimum utility for me across all issues. Since we are still very early in the negotiation (t=0.16), I will continue a slow concession strategy. Having fully conceded on my least important issue (issueA), I will make another small concession on my next least important issue (issueB) while holding firm on the most critical issues (issueC and issueD).

The opponent continues to repeat their initial offer of all 'valueA's without making any concessions. Since it is still early in the negotiation (t=0.18), I will continue my gradual concession strategy on my less important issues to encourage reciprocity. I will concede issueB down to valueB while firmly holding onto my most important issues, issueC and issueD, at valueF to protect my overall utility.

The opponent is holding extremely firm, repeating the exact same bid of `valueA` for all issues without any concessions. Because we are still early in the negotiation (t=0.2), I will make one final significant concession by fully yielding on my two lowest-priority issues (issueA and issueB) to match their requested `valueA`. I will, however, hold completely firm on my most critical issues (issueC and issueD) to protect my remaining utility, serving as a clear signal of where my flexibility ends.

The opponent is repeating the exact same offer (valueA for all issues) without making any concessions. Since it is still early in the negotiation (t=0.22), I want to demonstrate some flexibility while protecting my highest-value issues. I have fully conceded my lowest-weight issues (issueA and issueB), so I will now make a small concession on issueC to see if they are willing to reciprocate.

We are still early in the negotiation (t=0.24), and I have already made rapid concessions on my lower-priority issues, issueA and issueB, down to the opponent's preferred value. Because the opponent has not compromised from their starting position across any issues, I need to slow down my concessions and hold firm on my high-priority issues (issueC and issueD) to avoid unnecessarily dragging down my utility. I am slightly adjusting issueB to probe for alternative acceptable tradeoffs without sacrificing significant value.

The opponent has rigidly repeated their initial offer of all `valueA` across all 13 rounds so far. To elicit some movement and signal that I will not just unilaterally concede to their demands, I will retract some of my previous full concessions on my lower-priority issues (issueA and issueB) while exploring new utility-equivalent configurations. Since t=0.26, I have plenty of time and will firmly protect value on my highest-priority issues (issueC and issueD).

The opponent has remained completely firm on offering the exact same bid (valueA for all issues) throughout the entire negotiation. Since we are still early in the negotiation (t=0.28), I should not concede too quickly on my highest weighted issues. I will hold strong with valueF on issueD and valueE on issueC, while mixing intermediate values for issueA and issueB to find a combination that meets my target utility of ~0.82.

The opponent has repeatedly proposed the identical offer (`valueA` for all issues) across 15 turns without conceding anything. Given that the time fraction is only 0.3, I will hold my target utility firm around 0.8 and refuse to drop significantly further until they show some willingness to negotiate. I will offer a new combination that meets this threshold by keeping my most critical issue (issueD) maximized while finding compromises on less important ones.

The opponent is holding strictly to their initial bid across all issues. As we are approaching the mid-game (t=0.32), I need to continue making measured concessions to find a package they might accept while protecting my most critical issues. I will offer a combination that fully concedes on my least important issue (issueA) and makes a slight concession on issueC, while keeping issueB moderate and holding strict on my highest priority issue (issueD).

The opponent is holding entirely firm on their opening offer across all issues since the beginning of the negotiation. Since the time fraction is still early (t=0.34), I will slowly concede on the less critical issues (issueA, issueB, and slightly issueC) while maintaining maximum utility on issueD (my highest weight issue). I am continuing to explore combinations that may encourage the opponent to move, aiming for a utility of roughly 0.73 to demonstrate flexibility.

The opponent is holding entirely rigid at their opening offer consisting completely of 'valueA' across all issues. Since the deadline is still safely far at t=0.36, I will continue to hold mostly firm on my high-weight issues (issueD and issueC) while making minor varying concessions on my lower-weight issues (issueA and issueB). This offer lowers issueA slightly to 'valueD' while bringing issueB to the opponent's favorable 'valueA', keeping my total utility solidly above 0.70 to press them for reciprocating concessions.

The opponent is holding entirely firm on their initial offer of all 'valueA', which has the absolute minimum utility for me. As the time fraction is still at 0.38, I have room to maintain a strong position while searching for concessions that the opponent might value. I will offer a mix of mid-tier values for my less important issues (issueA and issueB, conceding slightly to valueC and valueB respectively) while retaining high-utility values on my most important issues (issueC and issueD) to keep my target utility above 0.7.

The opponent has rigidly held to their maximum likely utility offer (valueA for all issues) across all turns. With the time fraction at 0.4, I need to continue making reasonable concessions on my lower-weighted issues (like issueA and issueB) while preserving high values on my top priorities, particularly issueD (weight 0.4) and issueC (weight 0.3). I will propose a moderate variation yielding around 0.68 utility for me to test if the opponent will budge on any issue.

The opponent has stubbornly persisted with offering `valueA` for all issues without any concessions since the beginning. As we approach the middle of the negotiation (t=0.42), I will continue my gradual concession strategy to see if any movement can break the deadlock. I will maintain my most important issue (issueD) at `valueF`, but concede slightly more on issueB and issueA to offer a new combination that yields a utility of ~0.69, hoping it aligns with their priorities.

The opponent is holding completely firm on `valueA` for all issues without any concessions or messages. Given that we are at t=0.44, I will maintain a firm stance on my most critical issues (`issueD` and `issueC`) while offering moderate concessions on my less important issues (`issueA` and `issueB`) to explore potential tradeoffs that could break the deadlock. My target utility remains around 0.74 to continue showing flexibility without capitulating to their rigid strategy.

The opponent has rigidly held their initial offer of 'valueA' for all issues across every single round. To make progress as we approach the midpoint of the negotiation, I will concede completely to their demands on my two lowest-weighted issues (issueA and issueB) to demonstrate significant willingness to compromise. However, I will defensively hold firm on my highest-weighted issues (issueC and issueD) to maintain a reasonable overall utility.

The opponent has rigidly maintained the exact same offer (`valueA` for all issues) throughout the entire negotiation despite my numerous concessions on issues A, B, and C. Since we are approaching the midway point (t=0.48), I will now test their flexibility on issue D by making my first concession there (dropping to `valueE`). To maintain my overall utility near ~0.7, I will reclaim some value on issue C, offering a new combination to see if issue D is their primary motivation.

The opponent is uncompromising, persistently bidding valueA across all issues without sending any messages. At exactly the halfway mark (t = 0.5), I need to hold firm on my highest-weight issues (issueD and issueC) while searching for alternative packages. I will introduce a mid-range concession on my less important issues (issueA and issueB), along with moderate values on C and D, targeting a utility near 0.71 to test if they will finally depart from their initial stance.

The opponent is employing a rigid strategy, repeatedly offering the exact same combination (all valueA) without any concessions. As we have crossed the halfway mark (t=0.52), I will continue to make small, calculated concessions primarily on my lower-weight issues (issueB and issueA) to find a mutually acceptable point while firmly protecting my highest-value issues (issueD and issueC). I am adjusting issueB to valueB but holding issueC and issueD at valueE to maintain a strong overall utility.

The opponent has rigidly held to a single offer of 'valueA' for all issues throughout the entire negotiation, despite the time passing halfway. Given my preferences, their offer has very low utility for me. To seek a compromise while protecting my high-importance issues (issueC and issueD), I will fully yield on issueB to 'valueA' while maintaining 'valueE' for both issueC and issueD, resulting in a moderate utility reduction that gradually meets them on the lesser issues.

The opponent is remaining completely rigid, repeatedly offering 'valueA' for all issues despite my ongoing concessions. With time at t=0.56, I will continue to make steady, measured concessions on my lower-weighted issues (A and B) while slightly adjusting issueC to find a workable middle ground. However, I must protect my top-priority issueD to maintain a reasonable overall utility, targeting around 0.66.

The opponent is holding extremely firm on their completely one-sided opening bid of `valueA` across all issues, which happens to be my absolute lowest-utility outcome. Since we are in the mid-game (t=0.58), I need to show some willingness to compromise while still protecting my core interests. Given that I care least about `issueA` and `issueB`, I will concede those entirely to `valueA`, but slightly concede `issueC` to `valueD` and retain a high utility `valueE` for `issueD` to target a reasonable overall utility of 0.60.

The opponent has rigidly maintained the valueA position across all issues for most of the negotiation, but at t=0.60 they finally made a small concession on issueD (moving to valueB). Since issueD is my highest priority (0.4), I want to hold my high utility value (valueE) there while reciprocating their move by making a concession on another issue. I will decrease issueC to valueC to continue showing flexibility and edge toward an agreement, keeping utility above 0.55.

The opponent has rigidly requested `valueA` across all issues for most of the negotiation, but has just conceded on `issueD` to `valueB`. Since `issueD` is my most important matter, I will capitalize on their proven flexibility there by holding it at `valueE`. I'll lower my demand on `issueC` to `valueB` to offer a concession in return and keep the negotiation progressing toward a mutually acceptable agreement.

The opponent has stubbornly demanded 'valueA' across all issues for almost the entire negotiation, showing only a momentary concession on issueD before retracting it. To encourage cooperation while protecting my own utility, I will fully concede to their apparent target on my lowest-priority issues (issueA and issueB), while maintaining a firm stance on my most important issue (issueD) and offering a moderate compromise on issueC.

The opponent has rigidly held to `valueA` for `issueA`, `issueB`, and `issueC`, but recently showed flexibility by conceding `issueD` to `valueB`. Since `issueD` is my most important issue (weight 0.4) and `issueC` is my second most important (weight 0.3), I will accommodate their strong preference on my low-weight issues (`issueA` and `issueB` set to `valueA`) while holding `issueD` at my maximum value. I will slightly concede `issueC` to `valueC` to propose a mutually agreeable compromise that still maintains a strong overall utility for me (~0.62) as time approaches the late stages.

The opponent has rigidly demanded 'valueA' for all issues until recently, where they began slightly conceding on issueD to 'valueB'. I will continue offering 'valueA' for my lowest importance issues (issueA and issueB) as a show of good faith. To find an agreement before the deadline, I will step down my request on issueC to 'valueB', while maintaining my highest value 'valueF' on my most critical issue (issueD), targeting a moderate utility of around 0.56.

The opponent is holding firm on `valueA` across all issues, although they briefly conceded point `issueD` to `valueB`. Given my low issue weights on `issueA`, `issueB`, and `issueC`, I can concede those entirely to `valueA`. I will offer `valueF` heavily weighted toward `issueD`, as it holds 40% of my total utility structure, maintaining a total utility of 0.52 for me while providing the opponent exactly what they asked for on three of the four issues.

The opponent is extremely stubborn, holding firm mostly at their ideal `valueA` across all issues, with only very slight concessions on `issueD` (offering `valueB`). At t=0.72, time pressure is mounting. I have conceded on `issueA`, `issueB`, and `issueC` to accommodate them, but I must preserve my utility above 0.5 to make an agreement viable. I will offer a combination where I hold `issueD` at `valueF` and shift slightly on `issueB`, exploring whether they are more flexible there.

The opponent primarily demands `valueA` across all issues, but has just conceded slightly on `issueC` to `valueC`. Since `issueD` is my most crucial priority (weight 0.4), I will fully accommodate their requests on `issueA`, `issueB`, and `issueC` (matching `valueA` and `valueC`), provided they agree to `valueF` for `issueD`. This targets a utility of 0.616 and represents a fair compromise given the time remaining.

The opponent has rigidly held to valueA for most issues, but recently showed flexibility by proposing valueC for issueC. I will match their latest offer on issueA, issueB, and issueC. In exchange, I strongly need a high-utility value on issueD (my most important issue), so I will marginally concede to valueE, expecting an overall utility of approximately 0.55.

The time fraction is increasing to 0.78, so I need to make a solid compromise. The opponent has shown rigid preference for `valueA` on issues A and B, but has briefly accepted concessions proposing `issueC: valueC` and `issueD: valueB`. I will offer their preferred `valueA` on issues A and B (my lowest priorities) and their previously accepted `valueC` on issue C, while offering `valueD` on issue D to meet them halfway on my highest priority issue.

The opponent has shown flexibility on issueC (offering valueC) and issueD (offering valueB), though not at the same time. Realizing the deadline is approaching (t=0.8), I will concede further by combining their most favorable offer on issueC (valueC) with a moderate constraint on issueD (valueC), while remaining fully compliant with their preferred values for issues A and B (valueA). This brings my utility to ~0.42 without completely surrendering my highest-priority issue.

The opponent continues to demand valueA for issueB but has shown willingness to concede up to valueC on both issueC (at t=0.74) and issueD (in their latest offer). Since my last offer of A, A, C, C was countered with A, A, A, C, I will try a new combination by conceding further on issueC (to valueB) while demanding a slightly higher value (valueD) for issueD. This tests their exact tradeoff preferences ahead of the deadline while protecting my heavily-weighted issueD.
